name: Cython (Python-to-C Compiler)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); Stefan Behnel and contributors

performance.notes: Compiled C code; 10-100x faster than CPython for numeric loops; GIL control

agent_readiness.notes: Compile Python+static types to C for performance. Write .pyx files with type annotations: cdef int x = 5. cdef double[:] arr = np.zeros(100). def fast_sum(double[:] arr): cdef double s = 0; for i in range(len(arr)): s += arr[i]; return s. cython --annotate to see yellow/white (Python/C) regions. setup.py Extension with Cython.Build.cythonize(). GIL release: with nogil: parallel.prange(). Pure Python mode (.py with __cython__). Used by NumPy, lxml, scikit-learn, pandas internals. Cython 3.0 major release 2023.

gotchas: .pyx files require separate build step (not pure Python)
Debugging compiled code is harder than pure Python
Type annotations require learning Cython syntax (not standard Python types)
Build system integration adds complexity to packaging
best_when: Speeding up numeric Python loops 10-100x without rewriting in C
Calling C libraries with direct Cython bindings
Releasing the GIL for parallel computation in numeric code
Incrementally optimizing hot code paths in existing Python code
avoid_when: When Numba JIT is sufficient (no compilation step needed)
New C extension modules (pybind11/nanobind have better ergonomics)
Teams that can't maintain .pyx build infrastructure
summary: Cython compiles typed Python (.pyx) to C for 10-100x speedups on numeric loops. cdef int x declares C types; double[:] for typed memoryviews of NumPy arrays. with nogil: releases GIL for parallel code. cython --annotate shows Python vs C regions. Used by NumPy, lxml, scikit-learn internals. Requires build step (not pure Python). For JIT without compilation step, use Numba. For new C extensions, use pybind11 or nanobind.
